title: orobanche minor var. grisebachii
intro: Orobanche minor var. grisebachii, also known as Grisebach's broomrape, is a parasitic plant species belonging to the broomrape family (Orobanchaceae). Traditionally, Grisebach's broomrape has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties, offering potential health benefits in reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and fighting off infections. In traditional medicine, Grisebach's broomrape can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
